diagrams.title: Malware Classification Hierarchy
diagrams.description: A tree-like hierarchical diagram showing the main categories of malicious software and their subtypes, helping learners understand the taxonomy and relationships between different malware types.
elements: Primary branches: Viruses, Worms, Trojans, Ransomware, Spyware, Adware, Rootkits
Sub-branches for viruses (file virus, macro virus, boot sector virus)
Color-coded categories by severity or type
diagrams.purpose: Explains the taxonomy and classification of malware types, showing how different malicious software variants relate to each other and their common characteristics

diagrams.title: Malware Attack Lifecycle
diagrams.description: A horizontal flowchart depicting the typical stages of a malware attack from initial infection to damage/stealth, showing the progression and objectives at each stage.
elements: Initial Access (entry point icons: email, web, USB)
Arrows connecting sequential stages with labels
Optional: feedback loop showing adaptive malware behavior
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the complete lifecycle of a malware attack, helping security professionals understand where to implement defensive measures at each stage

diagrams.title: Malware Distribution Vectors
diagrams.description: A radial or spider diagram with a central 'Target System' and radiating vectors showing common methods malware uses to infect systems, with icons representing each attack vector.
elements: Radiating vectors: Email Attachments/Phishing, Malicious Websites, USB/Removable Media, Software Exploits, Social Engineering, Network Vulnerabilities, Fake Software Updates
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the various ways malicious software reaches and infects target systems, emphasizing the importance of layered security defenses
